finding.contract_claim: Parameter `error` is declared in the signature of log(message, error=False) (src/app/launcher.py line 1) as an accepted input that callers may pass.
finding.observed_behavior: The attributed call site, log("ok") in src/app/cli.py, does not pass `error`, so the parameter appears to always take its default False. `log` is a common name with high fan-out: src/noise.py defines an unrelated log(message) that is called locally.
finding.triage_reasoning: The claim calls `error` a dead parameter, but the function body on line 2 is `return f"{message}:{error}"` — `error` is unconditionally referenced in the return expression. A dead parameter is one that is declared but never read inside the function. Here it is read. The fact that the only observed call site omits the keyword argument is irrelevant; the default `False` is then substituted and included in the output. There is no gap.
